title: Patricia Amorim

infobox.value: (1969-02-13) February 13, 1969 (age 57)  [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro_(state) "Rio de Janeiro (state)"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil")

(born February 13, 1969, in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro")[[1]](#cite_note-nytimes-1)) is a [Jewish Brazilian](/wiki/Jewish_Brazilian "Jewish Brazilian")[[2]](#cite_note-2) former [Olympic](/wiki/Olympic_Games "Olympic Games") [freestyle](/wiki/Freestyle_swimming "Freestyle swimming") [swimmer](/wiki/Swimmer "Swimmer")[[3]](#cite_note-sportsreference-3) and former president of [Clube de Regatas do Flamengo](/wiki/Clube_de_Regatas_do_Flamengo "Clube de Regatas do Flamengo"). She is the first female president ever of the club, elected on December 7, 2009.[[1]](#cite_note-nytimes-1)[[4]](#cite_note-corinthiansu20-4)

["Presidente do Flamengo exalta sua origem judaica em encontro com jovens no Rio de Janeiro"](https://www.conib.org.br/presidente-do-flamengo-exalta-sua-origem-judaica-em-encontro-com-jovens-no-rio-de-janeiro/). 16 June 2011.

["Patrícia Amorim é eleita presidente do Flamengo"](http://globoesporte.globo.com/Esportes/Noticias/Times/Flamengo/0,,MUL1406867-9865,00-VENCEDORA+PATRICIA+AMORIM+PRETENDE+MANTER+ANDRADE+E+DEFINIR+PATROCINIOS.html) (in Portuguese). Globo Esporte. December 7, 2009. Retrieved December 8, 2009.

["Results at 1986 Madrid"](https://web.archive.org/web/20140407065846/http://www.usaswimming.org/_Rainbow/Documents/b8cad8e2-2559-4bf5-bdbc-e4d1718ec051/86_worldchamps%5B1%5D.pdf) (PDF). *USA Swimming*. 2013. Archived from [the original](http://www.usaswimming.org/_Rainbow/Documents/b8cad8e2-2559-4bf5-bdbc-e4d1718ec051/86_worldchamps%5B1%5D.pdf) (PDF) on April 7, 2014. Retrieved May 1, 2013.

["Results at 1987 Pan Am Games in Indianapolis"](https://web.archive.org/web/20120606215313/http://www.usaswimming.org/_Rainbow/Documents/ca27f27a-3d91-4248-bdab-1ab0dc7880e1/87_panam%5B1%5D.pdf) (PDF). *USA Swimming*. 2013. Archived from [the original](http://www.usaswimming.org/_Rainbow/Documents/ca27f27a-3d91-4248-bdab-1ab0dc7880e1/87_panam%5B1%5D.pdf) (PDF) on June 6, 2012. Retrieved May 1, 2013.

["Jewish Post 9 August 1989 — Hoosier State Chronicles: Indiana's Digital Historic Newspaper Program"](https://newspapers.library.in.gov/cgi-bin/indiana?a=d&d=JPOST19890809-01.1.20&e=-------en-20--1--txt-txIN-------). *newspapers.library.in.gov*.
["Brazilians learn to swim away"](http://www1.folha.uol.com.br/fsp/esporte/fk21039925.htm). *Folha de S.Paulo* (in Portuguese). March 21, 1999. Retrieved May 1, 2013.

["THE HISTORY OF THE RECORD OF 800 FREE FEMALE"](https://web.archive.org/web/20131105160418/http://www.bestswimming.com.br/conteudo.php?i=8093). *Best Swimming* (in Portuguese). March 9, 2008. Archived from [the original](http://www.bestswimming.com.br/conteudo.php?i=8093) on November 5, 2013. Retrieved May 1, 2013.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Patricia Amorim](/w/index.php?title=Patricia_Amorim&redirect=no "Patricia Amorim"))

Brazilian swimmer

Patricia Amorim

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Personal information | |
| Full name | Patricia Filler Amorim |
| Born | (1969-02-13) February 13, 1969 (age 57)  [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro"), [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro_(state) "Rio de Janeiro (state)"), [Brazil](/wiki/Brazil "Brazil") |
| Height | 1.65 m (5 ft 5 in) |
| Weight | 53 kg (117 lb) |
| Sport | |
| Sport | [Swimming](/wiki/Swimming_(sport) "Swimming (sport)") |
|  | |
| Strokes | Freestyle |
|  | |

**Patrícia Filler Amorim** (born February 13, 1969, in [Rio de Janeiro](/wiki/Rio_de_Janeiro "Rio de Janeiro")[[1]](#cite_note-nytimes-1)) is a [Jewish Brazilian](/wiki/Jewish_Brazilian "Jewish Brazilian")[[2]](#cite_note-2) former [Olympic](/wiki/Olympic_Games "Olympic Games") [freestyle](/wiki/Freestyle_swimming "Freestyle swimming") [swimmer](/wiki/Swimmer "Swimmer")[[3]](#cite_note-sportsreference-3) and former president of [Clube de Regatas do Flamengo](/wiki/Clube_de_Regatas_do_Flamengo "Clube de Regatas do Flamengo"). She is the first female president ever of the club, elected on December 7, 2009.[[1]](#cite_note-nytimes-1)[[4]](#cite_note-corinthiansu20-4)

She was born to a [bossa nova](/wiki/Bossa_nova "Bossa nova") bassist and a primary school teacher. When Amorim was 3 years old, her sister Paula who had asthma was recommended by a doctor to take up swimming to help with her asthma. Amorim started swimming by going along with Paula to the swimming classes. At 5 years old, Amorim swam across Guanabara Bay in Rio. She represented her native country at the [1988 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1988_Summer_Olympics "1988 Summer Olympics") in [Seoul, South Korea](/wiki/Seoul,_South_Korea "Seoul, South Korea"). She retired from swimming in 1991.[[1]](#cite_note-nytimes-1)

At the [1986 World Aquatics Championships](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1986_World_Aquatics_Championships "Swimming at the 1986 World Aquatics Championships") in [Madrid](/wiki/Madrid "Madrid"), Amorim finished 25th in the 200m freestyle, 24th in the 400m freestyle, and 20th in the 800m freestyle.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

She was at the [1987 Pan American Games](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1987_Pan_American_Games "Swimming at the 1987 Pan American Games"), in [Indianapolis](/wiki/Indianapolis "Indianapolis"), where she finished 4th in the 4×200-metre freestyle, and 5th in the 200-metre, 400-metre and 800-metre freestyle.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

At the [1988 Summer Olympics](/wiki/Swimming_at_the_1988_Summer_Olympics "Swimming at the 1988 Summer Olympics") in [Seoul](/wiki/Seoul "Seoul"), Amorim finished 11th in the 4×100-metre medley, 21st in the 800-metre freestyle, 24th in the 400-metre freestyle, and 25th in the 200-metre freestyle.[[3]](#cite_note-sportsreference-3)

Amorim won both the 400 m and 800 m freestyle gold medals at the [1989 Maccabiah Games](/wiki/1989_Maccabiah_Games "1989 Maccabiah Games") in Israel.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

Amorim broke the South American record of the 400-metre freestyle at the [1988 Summer Olympics](/wiki/1988_Summer_Olympics "1988 Summer Olympics"), with a time of 4:19.64. The record lasted 10 years, and was only broken in 1998 by [Nayara Ribeiro](/wiki/Nayara_Ribeiro "Nayara Ribeiro").[[8]](#cite_note-8)

Amorim also broke several South American records in the 800-metre free, the first in 1985 and the last in 1988 Seoul: 8:51.95. Her record lasted until 1999, when Ana Muniz did 8:48.53. She is the person who most often hit the Brazilian record of 800-metre freestyle, six times.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

Amorim is married to Fernando Sihman and has four children.[[1]](#cite_note-nytimes-1)

On December 3, 2012, in spite of running for Flamengo's presidency re-election, she was defeated by Eduardo Bandeira de Mello.
